key_insight: Combinatorial optimization provides a principled framework for making machine learning models more trustworthy by enabling global guarantees, certificates, and explicit trade-off analysis beyond standard heuristic methods.
engineering_relevance: For engineering ML systems, this survey shows how optimization-based tools can support model training, auditing, and deployment when transparency, robustness, fairness, privacy, or certifiability matter.
method: Survey CO-based methods for interpretable model learning, explanation generation, robustness analysis, fairness auditing, compression, and privacy.
Contrast optimization/certification approaches with gradient-based heuristics to highlight where formal guarantees are possible.
Synthesize training-time and post-training workflows that use solvers or hybrid algorithms for trustworthiness tasks.
evidence_signal: The paper is a survey synthesis rather than a new experimental study, and its claims are grounded in reviewing recent advances across multiple trustworthy ML tasks.
watch_item: Scalability remains a key concern, so exact optimization methods may require hybrid designs or approximations for large real-world models.
paper_title: Trustworthy Machine Learning through the Lens of Combinatorial Optimization: Survey and Research Perspectives
